reason: Modifying the FIPS cryptographic subpolicy can alter the set of allowed cryptographic algorithms, potentially breaking services that depend on specific algorithms (e.g., SSH, TLS) and may require a reboot to fully apply changes.
estimated_impact: reboot required; authentication/authorization affected; networking affected; May break cryptographic validation for services such as SSH, TLS, or PAM; Could cause authentication failures if modules are not compliant with new subpolicy; May require a system reboot or restart of affected services; Potential lockout if SSH keys or trusted certificates use disallowed algorithms
